content:
Currently we have a proliferation of macros due to the need of lock class keys.

I have a quite hacky POC that uses inline assembly to attach unique data per call site: https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=stable&mode=release&edition=2024&gist=059879a9f9d629d62f7fd207cc0b1b22.

I think this is too hacky to actually use in the kernel, but I'm throwing it here to invite ideas that would improve the ergnomics when initialization requires lock classes.
I'm guessing this is the idea I mentioned before?
Yes, this is the idea that we've discussed long time ago
This deduplicatoin stuff seems interesting. Can you say more?
I think we previously discussed if const ptr can improve this? I ended up not using const ptr as Location::caller() from const context is not the actual caller.

The deduplication thing is basically to ensure that if a function gets instantiated twice (e.g. into two crates because it's inlinable), the same instance is provided. If we just use the data without deduplication, then we have 2 lock class keys where we want one.
I think we might want a version that creates two callsites in that case
maybe
that was the behavior I intended when I suggested it at least
I guess for the lockdep use case it won't hurt to have duplicates, it just makes lockdep less efficient as it needs to track more classes
the other use-case I saw was kmalloc, which has some profiling feature per kmalloc callsite
the deduplication also seems unnecessary there
cool that you can merge them tho
Okay, perhaps we should introduce a version that doesn't dedup and place some caveat warnings there

Lina wrote https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/db615eab65ce7c47d07d683c6d8dc2b88e1ad45a for dynamic lockdep classes for the asahi driver. I dropped the change to lower the maintenance burden. I think it also depended on build system change to always use full absolute paths
There is a remap-path-prefix, although somehow in the build system we only have code to strip it, but nothing that adds it? :thinking:

Janne Grunau said:

Lina wrote https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/db615eab65ce7c47d07d683c6d8dc2b88e1ad45a for dynamic lockdep classes for the asahi driver. I dropped the change to lower the maintenance burden. I think it also depended on build system change to always use full absolute paths

This was discussed very early on. The vibe at the time is that this is too expensive as it needs to perform a lookup and allocation every time (even it's only used with lockdep enabled). With inline asm trick it'll be cheaper.
it passes absolute paths to the compiler https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/54ab88878869036c9d6620101bfe17a81e88c2f9

Janne Grunau said:

Lina wrote https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/db615eab65ce7c47d07d683c6d8dc2b88e1ad45a for dynamic lockdep classes for the asahi driver. I dropped the change to lower the maintenance burden. I think it also depended on build system change to always use full absolute paths

I think one issue of that is using Box to allocate lock keys dynamically (and automatically). In general we want to avoid calling allocation inside lockdep (the automatic lock keys is considered as an extension to lockdep).

Janne Grunau said:

it passes absolute paths to the compiler https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/54ab88878869036c9d6620101bfe17a81e88c2f9

That fixes the issue wrong way around, it should be like this:

diff --git a/rust/Makefile b/rust/Makefile
index 629b3bdd2b20..d87e3dd254bc 100644
--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -579,6 +579,7 @@ quiet_cmd_rustc_library = $(if $(skip_clippy),RUSTC,$(RUSTC_OR_CLIPPY_QUIET)) L
        $(filter-out $(skip_flags),$(rust_flags)) $(rustc_target_flags) \
        --emit=dep-info=$(depfile) --emit=obj=$@ \
        --emit=metadata=$(dir $@)$(patsubst %.o,lib%.rmeta,$(notdir $@)) \
+       --remap-path-prefix=$(abspath $(srctree))= \
        --crate-type rlib -L$(objtree)/$(obj) \
        --crate-name $(patsubst %.o,%,$(notdir $@)) $< \
        --sysroot=/dev/null \
diff --git a/scripts/Makefile.build b/scripts/Makefile.build
index 204e58dd1bb0..336f0670299f 100644
--- a/scripts/Makefile.build
+++ b/scripts/Makefile.build
@@ -330,6 +330,7 @@ rust_common_cmd = \
    -Zcrate-attr=no_std \
    -Zcrate-attr='feature($(rust_allowed_features))' \
    -Zunstable-options --extern pin_init --extern kernel \
+   --remap-path-prefix=$(abspath $(srctree))= \
    --crate-type rlib -L $(objtree)/rust/ \
    --crate-name $(basename $(notdir $@)) \
    --sysroot=/dev/null \
